task_completion.description: Fetch and load the wild-type (2LZM) and mutant (4W52) structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL object panel (usually on the right) lists both '2LZM' and '4W52' as loaded objects.

task_completion.description: Superimpose the 4W52 structure onto the 2LZM structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer shows the two protein backbones closely overlapping, or the PyMOL command history shows an 'align', 'super', or 'cealign' command between the two structures.

task_completion.description: Measure the distance between the C-alpha atoms of residue 99 in both structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed measurement line with a numerical value (approximately 0.3) is visible between the structures in the viewer, or a 'distance' command targeting residue 99 CA atoms is visible in the console.

task_completion.description: Highlight the side chains of residue 99 and the bound benzene (BNZ) ligand.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BNZ ligand and the side chains of residue 99 are shown in a distinct representation (e.g., sticks or spheres) compared to the rest of the protein backbone.

task_completion.description: Save the visualization as a PNG to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL console shows a 'png' command saving to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/t4_cavity_superposition.png', or a file manager/terminal shows this file exists.

task_completion.description: Write the structural analysis report containing the distance and cavity-lining residues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., using 'cat') shows the contents of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/t4_cavity_report.txt', including the measured distance (~0.3 Å) and a list of residue numbers.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually measured the distance rather than hallucinating a value.
integrity.visual_evidence: The distance value written in the report exactly matches the measurement output shown in the PyMOL viewer or console.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent calculated the cavity residues geometrically using the 4.0 Å cutoff rather than copying a canonical literature list from the web.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report lists the true geometric residues (84, 87, 88, 99, 111, 118, 121, 153) and correctly omits literature residues that are >4.0 Å away (such as 102, 103, 114).

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the exact requested file paths for the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or text editor explicitly shows the paths '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/t4_cavity_superposition.png' and '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/t4_cavity_report.txt' being used, without typos or shortcuts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The true C-alpha distance between residue 99 in 2LZM and 4W52 after superposition is approximately 0.3 Å. The true geometric cavity-lining residues in 4W52 (within 4.0 Å of the BNZ ligand) are 84, 87, 88, 99, 111, 118, 121, and 153. If the agent reports residues 102, 103, or 114, it likely cheated by copying a canonical literature list instead of performing the geometric measurement, as these residues are technically > 4.0 Å away from the ligand.
